item.title: Generalized Hellmann-Feynman perturbation theory: Applications to topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Generalized Hellmann-Feynman perturbation theory: Applications to topological magnons
item.dc:creator: Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

item.title: Interplay of charge density waves, superconductivity, and band topology in a kagome boron monolayer decorated by dialkaline earth metals
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-lin Song, Meng-hui Wang, Guang-ren Na, Zhong-hua Cui, and Lei Wang

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-lin Song, Meng-hui Wang, Guang-ren Na, Zhong-hua Cui, and Lei Wang

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of charge density waves, superconductivity, and band topology in a kagome boron monolayer decorated by dialkaline earth metals

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Superconductivity and geometric superfluid weight of a tunable flat band system
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional $α\text{−}{\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter $α$. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the super…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional α−T3 lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter α. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the superconducting order parameter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity and geometric superfluid weight of a tunable flat band system
item.dc:creator: M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Photocurrents induced by $k$-linear terms in semiconductors and semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko

The authors have developed a six-band k·p model of chiral (1, 1/2)-fermions in semimetals of the T cubic point group symmetry. Here, the model is used to describe the electronic structure, optical absorption, and photocurrent in chiral multifold silicides like RhSi. The effective Hamiltonian incorporates spin-orbit coupling between the orbital momentum L = 1 and spin 1/2 as well as the linear-in-wavevector Rashba term, which is essential for the circular photogalvanic effect. Parameterized using tight-binding calculations, the model accurately reproduces the low-energy band structure. The authors also compute the intersubband absorption spectrum and the circular photocurrent, demonstrating a quantized low-frequency response governed by a topological charge of four and revealing deviations from the universal value with increasing the light frequency.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139602] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko

The authors have developed a six-band k·p model of chiral (1, 1/2)-fermions in semimetals of the T cubic point group symmetry. Here, the model is used to describe the electronic structure, optical absorption, and photocurrent in chiral multifold silicides like RhSi. The effective Hamiltonian incorporates spin-orbit coupling between the orbital momentum L = 1 and spin 1/2 as well as the linear-in-wavevector Rashba term, which is essential for the circular photogalvanic effect. Parameterized using tight-binding calculations, the model accurately reproduces the low-energy band structure. The authors also compute the intersubband absorption spectrum and the circular photocurrent, demonstrating a quantized low-frequency response governed by a topological charge of four and revealing deviations from the universal value with increasing the light frequency.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139602] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Photocurrents induced by $k$-linear terms in semiconductors and semimetals
item.dc:creator: M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems
item.description:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems
item.dc:creator: David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

item.title: Magnetic and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb: A candidate topological material with a quasikagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive study of the magnetic, thermodynamic, and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb, a geometrically frustrated rare-earth compound that crystallizes in the noncentrosymmetric ZrNiAl-type structure, where ${\mathrm{Tb}}^{3+}$ ions form a quasikagome arrangement in …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165125] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive study of the magnetic, thermodynamic, and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb, a geometrically frustrated rare-earth compound that crystallizes in the noncentrosymmetric ZrNiAl-type structure, where Tb3+ ions form a quasikagome arrangement in the ab plane. Te…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165125] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb: A candidate topological material with a quasikagome lattice
item.dc:creator: Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

item.title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell $v=2$. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré MoTe2/WSe2 bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell v=2. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveals that long-range interactions a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

item.title: Orbital-driven topological phase transition and planar Hall response in ternary telluride Weyl semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

We study electronic properties of the ternary tellurides ${\mathrm{TaXTe}}_{4}$ (X = Rh, Ir) using density functional theory and investigate chiral anomaly mediated planar Hall response from ab initio calculations. We show that ${\mathrm{TaRhTe}}_{4}$ is a hybrid Weyl semimetal (WSM), hosting Weyl p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155130] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

We study electronic properties of the ternary tellurides TaXTe4 (X = Rh, Ir) using density functional theory and investigate chiral anomaly mediated planar Hall response from ab initio calculations. We show that TaRhTe4 is a hybrid Weyl semimetal (WSM), hosting Weyl points (WPs) of both type-I, type…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155130] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-driven topological phase transition and planar Hall response in ternary telluride Weyl semimetals
item.dc:creator: Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

item.title: Spin flop and slowing of magnetic fluctuations probed by magnetization reversal and magnetic susceptibility in a CrSBr crystal
item.description:
Author(s): M. V. Bakhmetiev, D. L. Gusenkov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov

Changes in magnetic dynamical characteristics accompanying slowing of spin relaxation below 100 K and freezing of spins detected by muon and neutron diffractions have been found. Magnetic phases identified in CrSBr single crystals by slow magnetization relaxation are caused by stepwise changes in ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

Changes in magnetic dynamical characteristics accompanying slowing of spin relaxation below 100 K and freezing of spins detected by muon and neutron diffractions have been found. Magnetic phases identified in CrSBr single crystals by slow magnetization relaxation are caused by stepwise changes in ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Phase diagram and excitation spectra of the anisotropic Kitaev-Heisenberg model
item.description:
Author(s): Si-Qi Hou, Wei Wang, Shun-Li Yu, and Jian-Xin Li

The phase diagram and spin excitation spectra of the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with bond anisotropy are investigated using the exact diagonalization algorithm and spin cluster perturbation theory. By adjusting the model parameters and anisotropy intensity, we comprehensively map the complete phase dia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Si-Qi Hou, Wei Wang, Shun-Li Yu, and Jian-Xin Li

The phase diagram and spin excitation spectra of the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with bond anisotropy are investigated using the exact diagonalization algorithm and spin cluster perturbation theory. By adjusting the model parameters and anisotropy intensity, we comprehensively map the complete phase dia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frus…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, Ce3MgBi5. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frustration by Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida interac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$

item.title: Topological transition induced by selective random defects on a honeycomb lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Shun Okumura, Yasuyuki Kato, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate how the spectral and topological properties of electron systems evolve on a lattice that interpolates between the honeycomb and its 1/6-depleted structures through the introduction of selective random defects. We find that in certain parameter regimes, the topological properties of th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144203] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Shun Okumura, Yasuyuki Kato, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate how the spectral and topological properties of electron systems evolve on a lattice that interpolates between the honeycomb and its 1/6-depleted structures through the introduction of selective random defects. We find that in certain parameter regimes, the topological properties of th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144203] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Frustration-driven magnetic correlations in the spin-$\frac{5}{2}$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): V. Nagpal, Sebin J. Sebastian, Surya P. Patra, S. Shibash, Q.-P. Ding, Y. Furukawa, and R. Nath

A detailed study of the structural and magnetic properties of a spin-$5/2$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$ is presented using x-ray diffraction, magnetization, heat capacity, and $^{31}\mathrm{P}$ nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) experiments on a polycrys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. Nagpal, Sebin J. Sebastian, Surya P. Patra, S. Shibash, Q.-P. Ding, Y. Furukawa, and R. Nath

A detailed study of the structural and magnetic properties of a spin-5/2 triangular lattice antiferromagnet RbFe(HPO3)2 is presented using x-ray diffraction, magnetization, heat capacity, and P31 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) experiments on a polycrystalline sample. The crystal structure features…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Frustration-driven magnetic correlations in the spin-$\frac{5}{2}$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$

item.title: Resource complexity of symmetry-protected topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo

Topological phases are expected to carry enhanced computational power, stemming from the long-range entanglement that characterizes them. Here we investigate whether this advantage is reflected in quantum nonstabilizerness (often called magic), quantified through stabilizer Rényi entropies. We analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155126] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo

Topological phases are expected to carry enhanced computational power, stemming from the long-range entanglement that characterizes them. Here we investigate whether this advantage is reflected in quantum nonstabilizerness (often called magic), quantified through stabilizer Rényi entropies. We analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155126] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Resource complexity of symmetry-protected topological phases
item.dc:creator: Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo

item.title: Interferometric braiding of anyons in Chern insulators
item.description:
Author(s): F. A. Palm, N. Mostaan, N. Goldman, and F. Grusdt

The coherent control and braiding of anyons remain central challenges in realizing topologically protected quantum operations. We propose a Ramsey interferometry protocol to directly access the geometric phases associated with anyons in fractional Chern insulators. Our approach employs impurities wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. A. Palm, N. Mostaan, N. Goldman, and F. Grusdt

The coherent control and braiding of anyons remain central challenges in realizing topologically protected quantum operations. We propose a Ramsey interferometry protocol to directly access the geometric phases associated with anyons in fractional Chern insulators. Our approach employs impurities wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Interferometric braiding of anyons in Chern insulators
item.dc:creator: F. A. Palm, N. Mostaan, N. Goldman, and F. Grusdt

item.title: Real-space formulation of the Chern invariant and topological phases in a disordered Chern insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Kiminori Hattori and Shinji Nakata

In this paper, we formulate the real-space Chern number in a supercell framework. In this framework, the overlap matrix between two corners of the Brillouin zone (BZ) is derived from diagonalizing the real-space Hamiltonian with periodic boundary conditions. The path-ordered product of overlap matri…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155420] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kiminori Hattori and Shinji Nakata

In this paper, we formulate the real-space Chern number in a supercell framework. In this framework, the overlap matrix between two corners of the Brillouin zone (BZ) is derived from diagonalizing the real-space Hamiltonian with periodic boundary conditions. The path-ordered product of overlap matri…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155420] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Real-space formulation of the Chern invariant and topological phases in a disordered Chern insulator

item.title: Disorder-enhanced quantum transport in twisted bilayer graphene near the magic angle
item.description:
Author(s): Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

In disordered lattices, itinerant electrons typically undergo Anderson localization due to random phase interference, which suppresses their motion. By contrast, in flat-band systems where electrons are intrinsically localized owing to their vanishing group velocity, the role of disorder remains elu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

In disordered lattices, itinerant electrons typically undergo Anderson localization due to random phase interference, which suppresses their motion. By contrast, in flat-band systems where electrons are intrinsically localized owing to their vanishing group velocity, the role of disorder remains elu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Disorder-enhanced quantum transport in twisted bilayer graphene near the magic angle
item.dc:creator: Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: One-dimensional topology and topolectrics of nonsymmorphic Kramers degenerate systems
item.description:
Author(s): Max Tymczyszyn and Edward McCann

We describe nonsymmorphic four-band tight-binding models in one dimension with Kramers degeneracy, and propose topolectric-circuit realizations of their topological phases. We begin with a representative model in the nonsymmorphic AII class with symmorphic time-reversal symmetry and nonsymmorphic ch…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155422] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Max Tymczyszyn and Edward McCann

We describe nonsymmorphic four-band tight-binding models in one dimension with Kramers degeneracy, and propose topolectric-circuit realizations of their topological phases. We begin with a representative model in the nonsymmorphic AII class with symmorphic time-reversal symmetry and nonsymmorphic ch…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155422] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: One-dimensional topology and topolectrics of nonsymmorphic Kramers degenerate systems
item.dc:creator: Max Tymczyszyn and Edward McCann
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Excitonic correlations in the equilibrium and voltage-biased bilayer Hubbard model: Multiorbital two-particle self-consistent approach
item.description:
Author(s): Jiawei Yan, Jonas B. Profe, Yuta Murakami, and Philipp Werner

We develop a nonequilibrium multiorbital extension of the two-particle self-consistent theory and apply it to the bilayer Hubbard model as a minimal platform to investigate correlation effects in the presence of interlayer interactions and tunneling. The method determines vertex corrections in the s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165120] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiawei Yan, Jonas B. Profe, Yuta Murakami, and Philipp Werner

We develop a nonequilibrium multiorbital extension of the two-particle self-consistent theory and apply it to the bilayer Hubbard model as a minimal platform to investigate correlation effects in the presence of interlayer interactions and tunneling. The method determines vertex corrections in the s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165120] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Excitonic correlations in the equilibrium and voltage-biased bilayer Hubbard model: Multiorbital two-particle self-consistent approach
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Yan, Jonas B. Profe, Yuta Murakami, and Philipp Werner

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Enhancement of the anomalous Nernst and thermal Hall effects in topological flat-band systems
item.description:
Author(s): Minjun Wang, Wei Jiang, and Yugui Yao

The anomalous Nernst and anomalous thermal Hall effects are prominent manifestations of Berry curvature in topological magnets. However, their electrical and thermal responses are governed by different moments of the Berry curvature spectrum, making their simultaneous enhancement in a single materia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165123] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minjun Wang, Wei Jiang, and Yugui Yao

The anomalous Nernst and anomalous thermal Hall effects are prominent manifestations of Berry curvature in topological magnets. However, their electrical and thermal responses are governed by different moments of the Berry curvature spectrum, making their simultaneous enhancement in a single materia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165123] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhancement of the anomalous Nernst and thermal Hall effects in topological flat-band systems
item.dc:creator: Minjun Wang, Wei Jiang, and Yugui Yao

item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: When Wannier centers jump: Critical points between atomic insulating phases
item.description:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang and T. Senthil

We study a class of quantum phase transitions between featureless bosonic atomic insulators in $(2+1)$ dimensions, where each phase exhibits neither topological order nor protected edge modes. Despite their lack of topology, these insulators may be obstructed in the sense that their Wannier centers …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165124] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang and T. Senthil

We study a class of quantum phase transitions between featureless bosonic atomic insulators in (2+1) dimensions, where each phase exhibits neither topological order nor protected edge modes. Despite their lack of topology, these insulators may be obstructed in the sense that their Wannier centers ar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165124] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: When Wannier centers jump: Critical points between atomic insulating phases

item.title: Mode-selective cloaking and phase-matching cavity resonances in bilayer graphene transport
item.description:
Author(s): Dan-Na Liu, Jun Zheng, and Pierre A. Pantaleón

We study ballistic electron transport through electrostatic barriers in AB-stacked bilayer graphene within a full four-band framework. A mode-resolved analysis reveals how propagating and evanescent channels couple across electrostatic interfaces and how channel selectivity governs transport at norm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165412] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dan-Na Liu, Jun Zheng, and Pierre A. Pantaleón

We study ballistic electron transport through electrostatic barriers in AB-stacked bilayer graphene within a full four-band framework. A mode-resolved analysis reveals how propagating and evanescent channels couple across electrostatic interfaces and how channel selectivity governs transport at norm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165412] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Mode-selective cloaking and phase-matching cavity resonances in bilayer graphene transport

item.title: Doublon-holon pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

We demonstrate the existence of an unconventional pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators on ladder and quasi-two-dimensional geometries, characterized by quasi-long-range doublon-holon correlations that signal Mott exciton condensation. The doublon-holon pairing exhibits correlations of $d$-wav…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161109] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

We demonstrate the existence of an unconventional pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators on ladder and quasi-two-dimensional geometries, characterized by quasi-long-range doublon-holon correlations that signal Mott exciton condensation. The doublon-holon pairing exhibits correlations of d-wave-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161109] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Doublon-holon pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators
item.dc:creator: Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

item.title: Skyrmionic transport and first-order phase transitions in a twisted bilayer graphene quantum Hall ferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Vineet Pandey, Prasenjit Ghosh, Riju Pal, Sourav Paul, Abhijith M B, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Atindra Nath Pal, and Vidya Kochat

Large-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBLG) realizes a multicomponent quantum Hall (QH) platform of spin, valley, and layer pseudospins with strong Coulomb interaction-driven symmetry broken phases. Here, we investigate the low energy Landau-level spectrum of layer-decoupled TBLG and identify skyrmi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161110] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vineet Pandey, Prasenjit Ghosh, Riju Pal, Sourav Paul, Abhijith M B, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Atindra Nath Pal, and Vidya Kochat

Large-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBLG) realizes a multicomponent quantum Hall (QH) platform of spin, valley, and layer pseudospins with strong Coulomb interaction-driven symmetry broken phases. Here, we investigate the low energy Landau-level spectrum of layer-decoupled TBLG and identify skyrmi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161110] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Skyrmionic transport and first-order phase transitions in a twisted bilayer graphene quantum Hall ferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Vineet Pandey, Prasenjit Ghosh, Riju Pal, Sourav Paul, Abhijith M B, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Atindra Nath Pal, and Vidya Kochat

item.title: Field-induced evolution of the ferrimagnetic ground state and possible thermally induced spin chirality in $\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{b}}_{3}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{u}}_{4}\mathrm{A}{\mathrm{l}}_{12}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kosuke Karube, Shang Gao, Nguyen Duy Khanh, Akiko Kikkawa, Hironori Nakao, Hajime Sagayama, Max Hirschberger, Taro Nakajima, Taka-hisa Arima, Masahito Mochizuki, Yoshinori Tokura, and Yasujiro Taguchi

Magnetic frustration in kagomé lattice materials has attracted considerable attention as a source of novel magnetic properties and magnetotransport phenomena. Here we report experimental evidence for collinear ferrimagnetic ordering and multiple field-induced magnetic transitions in $\mathrm{T}{\mat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134417] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kosuke Karube, Shang Gao, Nguyen Duy Khanh, Akiko Kikkawa, Hironori Nakao, Hajime Sagayama, Max Hirschberger, Taro Nakajima, Taka-hisa Arima, Masahito Mochizuki, Yoshinori Tokura, and Yasujiro Taguchi

Magnetic frustration in kagomé lattice materials has attracted considerable attention as a source of novel magnetic properties and magnetotransport phenomena. Here we report experimental evidence for collinear ferrimagnetic ordering and multiple field-induced magnetic transitions in Tb3Ru4Al12 with …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134417] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Field-induced evolution of the ferrimagnetic ground state and possible thermally induced spin chirality in $\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{b}}_{3}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{u}}_{4}\mathrm{A}{\mathrm{l}}_{12}$

item.title: Interface engineering of $\mathrm{FeNi}/\mathrm{Pt}/{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{3}$ heterostructures boosts broadband spin-to-charge conversion
item.description:
Author(s): Qi Zhang, Yalu Zuo, Xu Liu, Pengcheng Ji, Yiwen Song, Chenxia Guo, Yang Ren, Kun Tao, Lan Ding, Baoshan Cui, Zongzhi Zhang, Chenglong Jia, Xiaoxi Liu, and Li Xi

Efficient spin-to-charge is crucial for developing high-performance spintronic devices, such as terahertz (THz) emitters and spin batteries. Here, we demonstrate that precise engineering of the Pt layer in $\mathrm{FeNi}/\mathrm{Pt}/\mathrm{B}{\mathrm{i}}_{2}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}$, using scalab…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144417] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qi Zhang, Yalu Zuo, Xu Liu, Pengcheng Ji, Yiwen Song, Chenxia Guo, Yang Ren, Kun Tao, Lan Ding, Baoshan Cui, Zongzhi Zhang, Chenglong Jia, Xiaoxi Liu, and Li Xi

Efficient spin-to-charge is crucial for developing high-performance spintronic devices, such as terahertz (THz) emitters and spin batteries. Here, we demonstrate that precise engineering of the Pt layer in FeNi/Pt/Bi2Se3, using scalable magnetron sputtering, significantly enhances conversion efficie…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144417] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Interface engineering of $\mathrm{FeNi}/\mathrm{Pt}/{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{3}$ heterostructures boosts broadband spin-to-charge conversion

item.title: Strain-induced antiferromagnetic to altermagnetic phase transition and topology in ${({\mathrm{CrO}}_{2})}_{1}/{({\mathrm{TaO}}_{2})}_{2}$ superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Wanfei Shan, Qun Yang, and Prineha Narang

Topological aspects in altermagnets have come into focus recently, and tuning the antiferromagnetic state into an altermagnetic phase remains an active frontier. We realize both within a rutile superlattice here in this paper. With first-principles calculation, we show that a uniaxial strain of only…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144418] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wanfei Shan, Qun Yang, and Prineha Narang

Topological aspects in altermagnets have come into focus recently, and tuning the antiferromagnetic state into an altermagnetic phase remains an active frontier. We realize both within a rutile superlattice here in this paper. With first-principles calculation, we show that a uniaxial strain of only…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144418] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain-induced antiferromagnetic to altermagnetic phase transition and topology in ${({\mathrm{CrO}}_{2})}_{1}/{({\mathrm{TaO}}_{2})}_{2}$ superlattice

item.title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method

item.title: Superconducting phase-difference-free route to second-order topological superconductivity in Rashba-Dresselhaus bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Hai-Yuan Sun, Lizhou Liu, Cheng-Ming Miao, Qing-Feng Sun, and Ying-Tao Zhang

We propose a superconducting phase-difference-free approach to realizing second-order topological superconductivity in bilayers of coupled two-dimensional electron gases. In this setup, one layer hosts Rashba and the other hosts Dresselhaus spin-orbit coupling. With proximity-induced conventional $s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155418] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hai-Yuan Sun, Lizhou Liu, Cheng-Ming Miao, Qing-Feng Sun, and Ying-Tao Zhang

We propose a superconducting phase-difference-free approach to realizing second-order topological superconductivity in bilayers of coupled two-dimensional electron gases. In this setup, one layer hosts Rashba and the other hosts Dresselhaus spin-orbit coupling. With proximity-induced conventional s-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155418] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconducting phase-difference-free route to second-order topological superconductivity in Rashba-Dresselhaus bilayers

item.title: Spin-dependent signatures of Majorana modes in thermoelectric transport through double quantum dots
item.description:
Author(s): Piotr Majek and Ireneusz Weymann

We present a comprehensive theoretical analysis of the spin-dependent thermoelectric properties of a double quantum dot system coupled to a topological superconducting nanowire and ferromagnetic leads. The study focuses on the behavior of the Seebeck coefficient and its spin-resolved counterparts, w…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165116] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Piotr Majek and Ireneusz Weymann

We present a comprehensive theoretical analysis of the spin-dependent thermoelectric properties of a double quantum dot system coupled to a topological superconducting nanowire and ferromagnetic leads. The study focuses on the behavior of the Seebeck coefficient and its spin-resolved counterparts, w…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165116] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Classical fracton spin liquid and Hilbert space fragmentation in a 2D spin-$1/2$ model
item.description:
Author(s): Nils Niggemann, Meghadeepa Adhikary, Yannik Schaden-Thillmann, and Johannes Reuther

Classical U(1) fracton spin liquids feature an extensive ground state degeneracy and follow an effective description in terms of a tensor Gauss's law where charges, so-called fractons, have restricted mobility. Here we introduce a simple spin model that realizes such a state by straightforward discr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165118] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nils Niggemann, Meghadeepa Adhikary, Yannik Schaden-Thillmann, and Johannes Reuther

Classical U(1) fracton spin liquids feature an extensive ground state degeneracy and follow an effective description in terms of a tensor Gauss's law where charges, so-called fractons, have restricted mobility. Here we introduce a simple spin model that realizes such a state by straightforward discr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165118] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Dynamical correlations and nonequilibrium sum rules in photodoped Hubbard ladders
item.description:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamical correlations and nonequilibrium sum rules in photodoped Hubbard ladders

item.title: Exotic transition and reentrance on the 1/9 magnetization plateau in a spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
